item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: Contemporary Dance for Every Body
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: Seattle Central College Continuing Education, 1701 Broadway, Seattle, WA

Class Overview Would you like to cultivate body awareness through movement? Explore a grounded and natural approach to moving your body, led by a contemporary dance instructor. Through floorwork, movement sequences, and phrasing, this class cultivates body awareness. Explore efficiency in movement transitions. Develop a deeper understanding of how your body moves naturally in relation to the floor, emphasizing organic flow, continuity, spirality, softness, core engagement, and the intelligent use of gravity. Open your mind and body through guided movement! Explore points of support, releasing tension, and engaging the core to facilitate flow and prevent injury. Movement principles will draw from contemporary dance and somatic practices, encouraging dancers to move with clarity, sensitivity, and presence. This class is for dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners, and anyone interested in exploring movement in their bodies -- no experience necessary. Designed to be inclusive, this class offers both accessibility and challenge to movers at various levels and all backgrounds.  Learning Objectives Understand and embody key principles of movement floorwork, including gravity, spirality, and core initiation. Improve movement continuity and flow between levels \(floor to standing and vice versa\). Explore softness and efficiency through core engagement. Identify and use anatomical points of support for safe and fluid movement. Develop new dance tools Feel more connected with the possibilities of your body in a conscious way to live a more present, healthy, and aware life. Who Might Enjoy This Class? Adult learners of all backgrounds \(18\+\), including dancers, actors, physical theater artists, yoga/movement practitioners Anyone interested in embodied movement practices. Some prior movement or dance experience is helpful but not required. The class will be structured to accommodate varying levels of experience and physical ability.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: Rocketship, The Telephone Numbers, Remedy & Wren
item.description:
Thursday, April 16, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Rocketship “A Certain Smile, A Certain Sadness” 30th Anniversary Re-issue and West Coast Tour Some of the best records are those that transcend the usual parameters of music to create worlds of their own. When it’s clear that what you’re listening to isn’t just a collection of that year’s songs, but a deeply thought-out, architected space where ideas clash, converse, interlock; these are the albums that end up lasting well beyond their era, changing with us, and revealing new surprises every time we return to them. One of the clearest examples of an album that crafts a strange and beautiful world not quite like any other is Rocketship’s 1996 full-length debut A Certain Smile, A Certain Sadness. Even upon arrival, this was an experience unto itself, and 30 years later, these eight songs of bittersweet bliss still feel new. Slumberland is delighted to release a 30th Anniversary edition of this instant classic, making it readily available on vinyl and CD once again after years of unavailability and collector demand. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: Decay
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: The Vestibule, 5919 15th Ave. N.W., Seattle, WA

Decay explores how we navigate and engage with the inevitable entropy of our world. This exhibition shows us the joy of degeneration, the beauty of collapse, the peace that comes from crumbling into dust. The work, primarily from Pacific Northwest artists, ranges from sculptural, to wall-mounted 3D, video and installation works. Included in this exhibition will be works created using natural and reclaimed materials, as well as pieces designed to create spaces for quiet reflection on the theme. Decay appears as part of the natural world and in our bodies in many forms, and so over the duration of the show, some works may change over time, exemplifying the theme. Many of the pieces prompt reflection on mortality, the fragility of the physical body and the sacred.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Scott Gibson - Visual Symphony II
item.description:
Friday, April 17, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Gray Sky Gallery, 320 First Ave. S., Seattle, WA

In March, Visual Symphony II Scott Gibson invites viewers into a world where music and art collide. Each work is an exploration of the vivid, sensory experience of sound translating into vibrant, emotional visuals. As an artist with synesthesia each work expresses Scott’s personal perception of what music looks like. Scott uses resin techniques that he has pioneered and pushes the boundaries of this medium.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: Beyond Mysticism: The Modern Northwest
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

image: Drift No. 2, 1936, Malcolm M. Roberts, American, 1913-1990, tempera on board, 25 1/2 x 20 5/8 in., Seattle Art Museum, Eugene Fuller Memorial Collection, 37.103, © Malcolm M. Roberts, photo: Scott Leen. Inspired by the region’s natural beauty and rapidly changing city, this exhibition showcases artists who told the raw story of Seattle in the 20th century. By sharing the Pacific Northwest’s perspective through trending art styles—including Social Realism, Surrealism, and Abstract Expressionism—these artists offered a unique take on Modernist art and challenged the “mystic” label ascribed to their works. Spearheading the movement were local icons Mark Tobey, Kenneth Callahan, Guy Anderson, and Morris Graves—but the range and breadth of Pacific Northwest art goes beyond the “Big Four” of the Northwest School. Featuring more than 150 pieces, this exhibition showcases works that investigate the complexities of daily life in the region. Whether applying an ecocritical lens or producing dreamy and uncanny visuals, these artists expressed the duality of the natural world and city life to warn of the environmental and social impacts of industrialization. From Malcolm Roberts to Kenjiro Nomura, learn how Northwest artists applied their regional perspective to broader artistic developments to create a new form of American Modernism. The exhibition is accompanied by a fully illustrated catalogue authored by Theresa Papanikolas, SAM’s Ann M. Barwick Curator of American Art.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: TADAIMA: I'm Home
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Museum of History and Industry, 860 Terry Ave N, Seattle, Seattle, WA

After more than 80 years, a treasured collection returns “home” to Seattle’s Japanese American community. In 1942 dozens of traditional Boys’ and Girls’ Day dolls were entrusted to Bailey Gatzert Elementary School by Japanese American families facing forced incarceration. Most were never reclaimed after the war. Artist Miya Sukune uses recent research in MOHAI’s collection, as well as interviews with Japanese American survivors and their descendants, to create an installation called “Tadaima,” meaning “I’m home.” TADAIMA: “I’m Home!” Bringing Seattle’s Girls’ and Boys’ Day Dolls Home reconnects the dolls with their traditional role of protecting and blessing children. It invites visitors to celebrate the resilience, memory, and enduring traditions of the Japanese American Community.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

Event details

item.title: Samantha Yun Wall: What We Leave Behind
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Samantha Yun Wall’s new paintings use overlapping silhouettes of female figures as portals to unknown spaces and different temporal realms. Impetus for the new body of work is a Korean folk tale in which the Pasque flower is symbolic of a grandmother who passed away without the loving care of her grandchildren. It is a story of melancholy, loss, and remembrance. The delicate hair on the flower’s stem differentiates it, and the artist gives the plant a surreal aspect in some of her paintings, replacing the flower’s center with a watchful eye.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: Clock-Out Lounge Presents: The Messthetics and James Brandon Lewis w/ Ruminants
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge\, 4864 Beacon Ave S\, Seattle\, 98108\, United States Of America

Clock-Out Lounge Presents: The Messthetics and James Brandon LewisDeface the Currency Tourwith special guest RuminantsSat April 18thDoors 8:3021+$22adv/$25doshttps://themessthetics.bandcamp.com/album/the-messthetics-and-james-brandon-lewisDeface the Currency\, the new second collaborative album from the Messthetics andJames Brandon Lewis\, grew out of a simple intuition. The group with saxophonistLewis joining the core Messthetics lineup of Brendan Canty on drums\, Joe Lally on bass and Anthony Pirog on guitar was on tour in the summer of 2025 when Canty knew it was time to go back into the studio. This configuration of the band had debuted on record the year prior\, releasing an acclaimed self-titled album via Impulse!\, the legendary jazz label. But as the quartet logged serious time on the road\, the drummer felt their chemistry evolving\, so he called up engineer Don Godwin and booked a couple of days at Tonal Park in Takoma Park\, Maryland\, where The Messthetics and James Brandon Lewis was recorded.Deface the Currency preserves the tightness and variety of its predecessor\, but across the albums seven tracks\, it reveals new levels of confidence and risk. On pieces such as the title track named after a quote from Greek philosopher Diogenes that speaks to challenging societal norms and a new version of Serpent Tongue\, which first appeared on the Messthetics self-titled 2018 debut\, the quartet reaches ecstatic peaks that convey fun as much as ferocity. More than any earlier statement by the band\, the record seamlessly melds the sounds that make up its collective DNA\, from punk rock to free jazz and funk\, balancing compositional precision with palpable improvisational fire.Ruminants is a trio based in Portland\, OR and Tacoma\, WA featuring Marisa Anderson (Thrill Jockey recording artist\, frequent collaborator with Jim White\, Tara Jane\, ONeil\, William Tyler) on guitar\, Sam Coomes (Quasi\, Elliott Smith\, Jon Spencer\, Jandek\, Built To Spill s first official Dead Moon night\, the three wondered what would happen if they tried improvising together. A practice tape was made and then shelved for the next 7 years. In fall of 2025\, they decided to revisit the project and were delighted to discover theyd made some interesting noise together. Experimenting loosely with composition\, structure and improvisation\, the distinct styles of each player can be heard interplaying with a diverse range of themes.

From Clock-Out Lounge

item.title: Summer Cannibals, Divers, LEMON BOY
item.description:
Saturday, April 18, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $15 ($20.09 after fees) Day of: $18 ($26.27 after fees) Summer Cannibals: Summer Cannibals broke up. Three years later, they made a new album. More on that later. In the meantime, 2026 marks the 10-year anniversary of their acclaimed third record, Full Of It, released by NW punk mainstay Kill Rock Stars. To mark the anniversary, the band will play their first headlining shows since 2022, performing the album front to back in Portland at Mississippi Studios and Seattle at Sunset Tavern with longtime friends and Portland legends Divers. Full Of It, the album Pitchfork praised for “expertly balancing flame-belching Mad Max riffage with lyrics frankly exploring questions of codependence and need,” captured a turning point for the band, sharpening their raw, hook-driven sound into something more focused and intense. The anniversary is being marked with a vinyl repress, two Northwest shows, and a new album arriving later this year. Is this a victory lap? No, not really. It’s a reset, a return and whatever happens when you decide you aren’t actually finished. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Seattle Art Museum, 1300 First Avenue, Seattle, WA

Anila Quayyum Agha: Geometry of Light invites you to become part of the art. Agha, a Pakistani American artist, animates spaces with her large-scale sculptural installations. Suspended from the ceiling, Agha’s steel cubes are laser-cut with intricate designs that project geometric shadows onto the visitor. She draws on both the light and dark of her own life, using South Asian art practices to convey the gender discrimination she faced growing up as a young girl in Pakistan. An in-depth exploration of Islamic architecture, art, and identity, this is the first solo exhibition of a Pakistani American artist in SAM’s 90-year history. Agha is carving a name out for herself in the art world—visit the Seattle Asian Art Museum for an immersive and illuminating experience.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: Scott Gibson - Visual Symphony II
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: Gray Sky Gallery, 320 First Ave. S., Seattle, WA

In March, Visual Symphony II Scott Gibson invites viewers into a world where music and art collide. Each work is an exploration of the vivid, sensory experience of sound translating into vibrant, emotional visuals. As an artist with synesthesia each work expresses Scott’s personal perception of what music looks like. Scott uses resin techniques that he has pioneered and pushes the boundaries of this medium.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: CROSSROADS BELLEVUE INVITES COMMUNITY TO ‘LIVE MUSIC AT THE MARKET STAGE
item.description:
Sunday, April 19, 2026 at 12:30 PM

Location: CROSSROADS BELLEVUE, 15600 NE 8th Street, Bellevue, WA

Crossroads Bellevue, a one-of-a-kind shopping, dining, and entertainment destination in the heart of Bellevue, Washington \(https://crossroadsbellevue.com\), invites the community to enjoy “Live Music at the Market Stage,” a series of free live musical performances held in the public market every Friday \(7 p.m. to 8:45 p.m.\), Saturday \(7 p.m. to 8:45 p.m.\), and Sunday \(12:30 p.m. to 2:30 p.m.\) now through the end of the year. Performances include an eclectic mix of acoustic guitar, Cuban music, jazz, local choirs and more. All performances are free of charge. To learn more about each upcoming performance, visit Market Stage | Crossroads Bellevue. While enjoying the music, guests are encouraged to dine at one of the center’s many restaurants. For more information about Crossroads Bellevue, visit https://crossroadsbellevue.com.

From SEAtoday - Arts & Culture

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Tuesday, April 21, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Fruition - Labor of Love 10th Anniversary Celebration!
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $30 ($37.08 after fees) Day of: $35 ($42.23 after fees) Fruition: Three songwriters. Five bandmates. More than 15 years together, building a grassroots audience with a combination of stacked vocal harmonies and collaborative, song-driven Americana. Fruition is proof that there’s strength in numbers. 10 years ago, Fruition released Labor of Love into the world. 10 years, hundreds of shows, and thousands upon thousands of miles later they are bringing it back to where it was born: the PNW. Join Fruition for one intimate night to celebrate! Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

Event details

item.title: KEXP's Roadhouse Presents: Emily Nenni w/ Jesco Payne & The Painkillers
item.description:
Thursday, April 23, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

Emily Nenni and Movin Shoes is all about the right fit. Made in Memphis, the Nashville based singer-songwriters fourth full length album is as much about dancing as following the steps of understanding and respect. Nennis songs reflect her solicitude, but are much more soulful in this setting - taking inspiration from the many impassioned performers of the region, to 60s girl groups and honky tonk heroes.Pairing once again with producer John James Tourville (The Deslondes), Movin Shoes was cut at Matt -Ross-Spangs new Southern Grooves studio. The 13 tracks tie up nicely with added covers from Paul Simon, Delbert McClinton and Cass Elliott. Movin Shoes will be released on May 1st via New West Records. This event is 21 and over. Any ticket holder unable to present valid identification indicating that they are at least 21 years of age will not be admitted to this event, and will not be eligible for a refund. This ticket is for admission to a live music venue. It provides the holder to observe a musical performance and nothing else. Other goods and services may be purchased once inside the venue. Please note, seating is limited and is available on a first come, first served basis. Price: $25.08 - $25.08

From Tractor Tavern

item.title: CL 102: Portable Lighting
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

If you're interested in running and hosting shows in unconventional locations, this is the class for you! In this interactive 2-hour workshop, students will learn the in’s and out’s of installing and programming lights for live events outside of the venue! Participants will explore areas such as installing light trees, cable management, programming lights, light design and placement, and more. This class is great for those who are interested in improving their concert lighting skills, especially for DIY shows or outdoor spaces.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

item.title: RS 101: Intro to Vera's Recording Studio
item.description:
Saturday, April 25, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Get into VERA’s Recording Studio through this 2-hour workshop and studio orientation! Learn the studio infrastructure including, bootup, signalflow, patchbay setup, mixing desk operation, and basic Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) integration. During this session, participants will experiment with different ways of running signals from the live room to the computer. Some familiarity with Digital Audio Workstations (i.e. ProTools, Logic, Reaper, Ableton, etc.) is encouraged. After taking this course, students who are new to studio recording should be ready for the multi-week Recording Studio Workshop Series, as well as having a better grounding for independent research. Students who are experienced with studio recording may be credentialed for volunteering at studio sessions.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Jonny Fritz w/ Sean Forth
item.description:
Monday, April 27, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Jonny Fritz: Jonny Fritz – a veteran of 15 years as a touring musician. Born in Missoula, Montana and raised in Esmont, Virginia. A near perfect blend of country and western, yet impossible to know what to call it or do with it. Jonny calls it Dad Country; a theme park tailor made for dad’s. That’s his genre (so he says) and if that makes sense to you then you ought to have your head checked. The guy does it all. He is a famed leatherworker known for covering household items in hand- tooled leather (from microwaves and coffee grinders to telecasters). He’s made guitar straps for all your favorite bands and he even co-wrote the hit song All Your Favorite Bands (with Taylor Goldsmith of Dawes). Currently residing in the foothills of Los Angeles, he hasn’t toured much in the past few years since focusing on selling houses and raising his daughter. He’s branded himself as L.A.’s Only RealtorTM and has become quite successful at selling multi-million dollar properties to the stars. Not to mention he’s the Music Director of a boutique music venue in Sonoma County, CA called Little Saint that has garnered some major headlines for their surprise shows featuring the likes of boygenius, Phoebe Bridgers, Rufus Wainwright, Hiss Golden Messenger, Lavender Country and so many more. There’s a special freakiness Fritz brings to whatever he does. Selling real estate, running marathons, songwriting, or leather-working. It somehow all makes sense once you learn to make sense of it. Catch this delicate flower the next time you smell him coming! You won’t want to miss out on your chance to see him do his thing. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

Event details

item.title: Earlybirds Club
item.description:
Friday, May 1, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: The Crocodile, 2505 1st Ave, Seattle, WA

Earlybirds Club is a dance party designed for women, trans, and non-binary folks that starts at 6pm and ends at 10pm because we have sh*t to do in the morning!At 2000s Night, were bringing peak Y2K energy: flip phones out, low-rise trauma healed, and the hits that defined a generation. From Britney to Beyonc, and from OutKast to Missy Elliott this is a scream-sing, drop-it-like-its-hot, why do I still know every word? situation.Earlybirds Club is more than a night out its a judgment-free, joy-forward space where kindness, good vibes, and sensible footwear rule. Come as you are (Juicy tracksuits welcome, comfy kicks encouraged) theres plenty of room on the dance floor for everyone.10% of ticket proceeds benefit Refugee Artisan Initiative, which partners with refugee and immigrant women to build skills, launch small businesses, and craft sustainable products that empower communities and protect our planet. Note: By purchasing these tickets, you are consenting to be photographed for promotional purposes. People of all backgrounds and identities are encouraged to attend all of our events. This event is 21 and over. Any ticket holder unable to present valid identification indicating that they are at least 21 years of age will not be admitted to this event, and will not be eligible for a refund. All sales are final. There are no refunds unless the event is cancelled or postponed Opening Acts are subject to change or cancelation at any time without notice. No refund will be owed if an Opening Act is changed or canceled. Doors times subject to change Tickets may not be resold or offered for resale. If we suspect tickets have been purchased for resale purposes only, we reserve the right to void or cancel the tickets without refund. Tickets obtained from unauthorized sources may be invalid, lost, stolen or counterfeit and if so, are void. Price: $42.3 - $42.3

From The Crocodile

item.title: GRINDHAUS with Bosco, Naomi Smalls, Aja, King Molasses, & Special Guests
item.description:
Friday, May 1, 2026 at 10:30 PM

Location: The Crocodile, 2505 1st Ave, Seattle, WA

This May 1-2, join the action with another double-feature installment of Seattles best drag and performance art showcase. International woman of mystery, showgirl, producer, and star of RuPauls Drag Race All Stars 10, Bosco, invites you into a world of drag excellence. You know the name, you know the number... Welcome to GRINDHAUS.Joined by Drag Race legends Naomi Smalls (RPDR S8, AS4) and Aja (RPDR S9, AS3, AS10), and Drag King royalty King Molasses (Winner of RevryTVs King of Drag S1), plus a dynamite cast of Seattle stars...Our hometown heroine, Bosco, presents two nights of electrifying stunts, explosive glamour, and high-octane thrills. The world is not enough, but Seattle is!Merging the raw excitement and hilarity of classic Drag revues, the glamour of burlesque and cabaret shows, and a bit of performance art for your nerves, GRINDHAUS is an unforgettable experience with your host Bosco at the wheel.Line Up:BoscoNaomi SmallsAjaKing MolassesCali ColbyClara VoyanceMiss Texas 1988 This event is 21 and over. Any ticket holder unable to present valid identification indicating that they are at least 21 years of age will not be admitted to this event, and will not be eligible for a refund. All sales are final. There are no refunds unless the event is cancelled or postponed Opening Acts are subject to change or cancelation at any time without notice. No refund will be owed if an Opening Act is changed or canceled. Doors times subject to change Tickets may not be resold or offered for resale. If we suspect tickets have been purchased for resale purposes only, we reserve the right to void or cancel the tickets without refund. Tickets obtained from unauthorized sources may be invalid, lost, stolen or counterfeit and if so, are void. Price: $34.82 - $96.08

From The Crocodile

item.title: CL 101: Intro to Concert Lighting
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

In this 2-hour workshop, students will have a practical grasp on lighting concepts and console operation, learn about different lighting instruments, and gain hands-on experience programming lights for Vera’s mainstage. Participants of the class will also gain basic fundamental knowledge of working with artists and production staff to elevate the concert experience as a lighting engineer. Complete this class to be eligible for Lighting Engineer volunteer shifts at Vera shows. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other production-related opportunities!

From The Vera Project

item.title: LS 101: Basics of Live Sound
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Basics of Live Sound is a 2-hour introduction to live audio engineering and an orientation to Vera Project’s methods and equipment. Topics covered include an introduction to microphones and mic placement, signal paths, stands, cables, DI’s, snake, sub-snakes and etiquette for working with bands. This class is designed for both the novices and experts of all ages and will give an overview of signal flow and setting up small PAs, in addition to the basic requirements for being part of the Vera sound team at shows. Discover why the accommodating, open minded “Vera Attitude” of show production is appreciated worldwide! This class is a prerequisite for advanced audio classes at Vera and Sound Runner shifts at Vera Project show. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships!

From The Vera Project

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Earlybirds Club
item.description:
Saturday, May 2, 2026 at 5:30 PM

Location: The Crocodile, 2505 1st Ave, Seattle, WA

Earlybirds Club is a dance party designed for women, trans, and non-binary folks that starts at 6pm and ends at 10pm - because we have shit to do in the morning!Our DJ spins music from the 80s to 2000s, including pop, hip-hop, new wave, and Rs home! Earlybirds Club is more than a fun night out, its a supportive space where kindness, good vibes, and sensible footwear rule, and 10% of proceeds always benefit a local non-profit. Come as you are - theres plenty of room on the dance floor for everyone.10% of ticket proceeds benefit Womens Reproductive Rights Assistance Project, which assists women who are financially unable to pay for safe, legal abortions or emergency contraception.Note: By purchasing these tickets, you are consenting to be photographed for promotional purposes. People of all backgrounds and identities are encouraged to attend all of our events. This event is 21 and over. item.title: Maye & Ambar Lucid
item.description:
Sunday, May 3, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: The Crocodile, 2505 1st Ave, Seattle, WA

Entre Dos Mundos brings together maye and Ambar Lucid for a tour rooted in duality; not just sonically, but also emotionally. Together, they create a space that feels both intimate and expansive, where softness, healing, and self-expression take center stage.Framed as a space to unmask and reconnect, the tour leans into themes of vulnerability and the divine feminine, inviting audiences to tap into a more instinctive and emotional side of themselves. While maye and Ambar come from distinct artistic worlds, their contrast is what defines the experience: two energies in conversation, balancing light and shadow in real time. All sales are final. item.title: Happy Landing, Lostboycrow
item.description:
Tuesday, May 5, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $22 ($28.33 after fees) Day of: $25 ($31.42 after fees) Happy Landing: HAPPY LANDING is an American band formed in Oxford, Mississippi in 2020 by Matthew Hendley (lead vocals, guitar), Keegan Christensen (vocals, keys), Jacob Christensen (drums, vocals), Andrew Gardner (fiddle, vocals), and Wilson Moyer (bass/guitar, vocals). The group pioneers a new wave of folk music, captivating audiences internationally with their energetic live shows and fresh sound, blended from the roots of southern rock, punk and folk. After debut festival appearances at Bonnaroo, Lollapalooza, and Summerfest, the band’s sophomore album, Big Sun, comes in 2026 paired with a major headline tour of the US & Canada. item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Wednesday, May 6, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Sam Blasucci, guests
item.description:
Thursday, May 7, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

$15 ($20.09 after fees) Sam Blasucci: Physical Dream is Sam Blasucci’s fourth studio album and first double LP. It’s a sensorial, immersive record that shimmers, shudders, and grooves with genre-blurring mastery. The 28-track album unfolds like a living body—nonlinear and dynamic, alive with cascading instrumentals, buoyant boogie rhythms, and a sound that is simultaneously groovy and poetic. Recorded over just two weeks on a remote island property in Sooke, British Columbia, Physical Dream was born close to the ground. Blasucci slept in a small shack on the land while working closely with engineer and collaborator Jonny Payne. Additional recording took place in studios across Los Angeles and Ojai, California. The album includes contributions from Mia Wilson (vocals), Randal Fisher (horns, flute), Andres Renteria (percussion), Laena Myers (violin, viola, vocals), and Leng Bian (harp). Aside from traditional instrumentation, the album also gives voice to the environments where the music was made. Night sounds recorded on Blasucci’s Ojai property appear on “WHO LIVES ON THESE LANDS.” Running water from a Big Sur waterfall appears on “ORCHIDS.” “SI SE PUEDE” includes audio recorded at an anti-ICE protest in Oxnard, California. These elements expand the album’s sense of space and relationship. Blasucci’s voice is deeply personal and human, yet constantly in dialogue with a wider living world—and asking it to dance. Lyrically, Physical Dream holds romance, mysticism, and political resistance side by side. Musically, the album feels like Leonard Cohen, Prince, and Brian Eno gave birth to a bilingual, fantastical, hypnotic phantom dance baby. Animated by introspection and motion, Physical Dream invites listeners into reflection and movement in equal measure. Born in Northeast Los Angeles, Blasucci has lived across the U.S. and Mexico, including Coahuila, New Orleans, and Orem, and now finds home in Ojai, California. He learned music from his father as a child and formed Mapache at age 20, releasing 5 albums before launching his solo career in 2023. Physical Dream will be released this spring on vinyl and all streaming platforms. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Friday, May 8, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

item.title: SP 201: Poster Printing
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Show posters, art prints, and flat-stock paper print projects galore! In this hands-on workshop, students will build on the basics of paper-based printing by practicing with a two-color design, specifically designed for the Poster Printing class. Learn concepts such as the basics of color separation, print registration, proper equipment use, and helpful tips to successfully run a series of prints in a DIY or professional studio. Pre-requisites: Students must complete Screenprint 101 before attending this class. The Vera Project does not provide refunds nor reschedule tickets for other class dates.

From The Vera Project

Event details

item.title: LS 201: Small PA Mixing
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

The Small PA Mixing workshop is designed to familiarize students with running a small powered speaker system. Small PAs are generally used for band practices, acoustic sets, DJ sets, poetry readings, protests, or even house shows and shows in Vera’s gallery space!. Topics will include: speaker amplification and setup, practicing cable management and signal flow, using EQ to ‘ring out’ a room, and mixing for small room shows. You must complete LS 101: Basics of Live Sound before you can take this class. This class is a prerequisite for the Front of House Mixing class at Vera Project shows and access to VERA’s PA rental system. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships.

From The Vera Project

item.title: SIr Woman - Spring Tour 2026 w/ Oh He Dead and Uncle Roy & Spice
item.description:
Saturday, May 9, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: The Crocodile, 2505 1st Ave, Seattle, WA

Were bringing the magic back. Sir Woman heads out in Spring 2026 with a full band for a run of shows built around feel, groove, and real-time energy.Fronted by Kelsey Wilson - co-founder of Wild Child and member of Glorietta - Sir Woman blends gospel-influenced soul, Rs warm, physical, and alive. The full band brings weight and swing, creating shows that feel loose but intentional, intimate but loud.Praised by NPR as a brave new R Sir Womans live performances are about being in the room when it happens - when the band hits, the groove settles in, and the night takes on a life of its own. item.title: Clock-Out Lounge Presents: Collide-O-Scope
item.description:
Monday, May 11, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge\, 4864 Beacon Ave S\, Seattle\, 98108\, United States Of America

Clock-Out Lounge Presents: Collide-O-ScopeEvery 2nd Monday!Doors 7pm21+ $15https://www.collideoscopeseattle.com/For 16 years\, Collide-O-Scope has been slaying Seattle audiences with its singular brand of entertainment keenly curated vintage vid features fabulous free prize drawings throughout the night!For a decade\, Collide-O-Scope had its home at Seattles beloved Re-bar\, until 2020\, when they took the show online while the world worked some stuff out. Re-emerging in 2022\, they made The Here-After (at The Crocodile) their home for the next four years. Now\, celebrating their 16th anniversary\, Collide-O-Scope has landed right here at Clock-Out Lounge\, and YOU can see them HERE every 2nd Monday! Collide-O-Scope is the greatest show anywhere! Dina MartinaHalf video art installation and half stoner gawkfest David Schmader

From Clock-Out Lounge

item.title: Raised By TV Events Presents: Seinfeld Trivia Night
item.description:
Tuesday, May 12, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge\, 4864 Beacon Ave S\, Seattle\, 98108\, United States Of America

RAISED By TV brings you:SEINFELD Trivia Night!50 BRAND NEW questions about your favorite NY misfits- covering all 9 Seasons Tues May 12th$7 ONLINE ADVANCE: {plus ticketing fees}$9 Day of and AT THE DOOR {plus ticketing fees}Doors open at 5:30. Trivia starts at 7pmTables are first come first serve. THE CLOCK-OUT LOUNGEIs a 21 and over venue after 8pm **PRIZES**The RAISED BY TV gang have put together some killer prize packages. Teams of 1-5 are welcome. Prizes for 1st\, 2nd\, t have to be good at trivia just come on down to relive some Jerry apt shenanigans with us. **FOOD & DRINKS**The Clock-Out has bar and full Stevies Famous Pizza food service; one of the things that makes it our favorite place.The show is available on NetflixWe hope you can join RbyTV for a safe night of fun and brains.Brandon Ryan

From Clock-Out Lounge

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, May 13, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

From The Vera Project

Advance: $15 ($20.09 after fees) Day of: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Asher White: As a multi-instrumentalist and producer, Asher White’s auteurish pop vision is vulgar and transcendent: marrying skittering noise with swooping strings, lively banjo with blasts of feedback, dissonant flutes with elegant bossa nova. Since the early 2010s she has developed a sprawling discography that spans experimental drone, tropicalia, noise rock, and folk. Advance: $22.50 ($30.90 after fees) Day of: $25 ($33.48 after fees) Coma Cinema: Coma Cinema is the longest running project of songwriter and producer, Mathew Lee Cothran, known for his work as Elvis Depressedly and The Goin’ Nowheres. Coma Cinema has been active since 2005, remaining independent and happily obscure in an ever dulling world. Coma Cinema is committed to fighting entropy by the alchemy of art, bringing thoughts to life in Cothran and his listener’s minds with a spirit of deep gratitude for those who have listened to and shared his music for many years.

item.title: Clock-Out Lounge Presents: Dazzling Killmen w/ Point Line Plane\, Quid Quo
item.description:
Friday, May 15, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge\, 4864 Beacon Ave S\, Seattle\, 98108\, United States Of America

Clock-Out Lounge Presents:Dazzling Killmen Point Line PlaneQuid Quo Fri May 15th8:30 doors 21+ $22adv/$25dos https://dazzlingkillmen.bandcamp.com/musicIn 1994\, Dazzling Killmen\, a quartet from the St. Louis area named after a line inanobscure\, grotesque 1963 short story by Lucas Samaras\, put out a record called Face of Collapse\,their second and final full-length. By the following year\, they had broken up. Face of Collapseis a milestone for underground extreme rock in the 90s and should be experienced by fans of everything from Slint to the Dillinger Escape Plan to Rush .In fact\, there would be no Converge\, Today is the Day or Dillinger Escape plan without this essential blueprint. As former Rolling Stone senior editor Hank Shteamer wrote \,No other music that I know of candeliver what this [music] delivers\, either emotionallyits specific combination of creeping dread\, frantic anxiety and seething rageor sonically: The grand\, gothic power chords at the outset\, sounding like some horror-movie overture. The thresher-like riff that follows\, and then\, the onset of one of the greatest sequences of aggressive rock music Ive ever heard: a sort of hypnotic death waltz…broken up by racing\, scampering full-band interludes and giving way to a deranged\, writhing climaxwith the guitars stabbing at oblique angles over the rhythm sections lockstep convulsionsthat feels at once vise-tight and completely unhinged. When the whole band kicks into the next break neck triplet riff\, the effect is one of complete information overload. For the first time in 30 years\, youre going to be able to hear this music live.https://pointlineplane.bandcamp.com/album/smoke-signalsBetween 2002 and 2005 Portland synth/punk duo Point Line Plane played 150 shows across the US. Vocalist/keyboardist Joshua Blanchard (Major Hex) and drummer Nathan Carson (Witch Mountain) initially honed their near-telepathic live skills in destructively short 20-minute concert performances. By the time theyd derezzed\, their discography included two studio albums\, the soundtrack score for a documentary film about haunted houses\, plus a slew of comp tracks and singles.The band made its name by creating noise music with pop hooks. As electro continued to fizzle out and screamy dance-punk/neo-no wave enjoyed a brief spike of popularity\, kids and college radio DJs took note of PLPs eclectic and original style\, often contrasting the sound to other innovators like Liars\, Ex-Models\, and Lightning Bolt. Actual comparisons were hard to draw\, however; reviews in print and online found writers grasping for metaphors rather than listing influences. Now\, Point Line Plane is rebooting. 2026 will see the original duo resume activity after having powered down 22 years ago with a slate of Northwest gigs planned in support of their SKiN GRAFT Records labelmates Dazzling Killmenplus a special appearance at the celebrated Caterwaul Fest!https://quidquo.bandcamp.com/music

From Clock-Out Lounge

item.title: Jonah Marks (Album Release: POEMS) w/Special Guests
item.description:
Friday, May 15, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $15 ($20.09 after fees) Day of: $20 ($26.27 after fees) Jonah Marks: Jonah Marks is an independent singer-songwriter and rapper crafting soulful, story-driven lyrics through Hip Hop and R&B. After spending more than a decade working behind the scenes in artist management and concert production, Jonah Marks steps fully into his own creative voice on POEMS, a 10-song narrative that challenges modern disconnection and overreliance on technology. The album opens with “Poems Over Cell Phones,” a thesis-driven track that sets the tone for a journey away from constant digital output and toward presence and creativity. As the record unfolds, its production becomes increasingly organic and acoustic, culminating in the community-focused closing track, “People Over Poems.” POEMS is primarily produced by Elan Wright, with additional production from Heavywait, Noah Coinflip, and Damono. The album features rich live instrumentation, including piano, strings, percussion, choir vocals, and a guest feature from hip-hop artist Rell Be Free. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Tuesday, May 19, 2026 at 4 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

Brought to life in the small Southern Ontario town of Elmira, Chris took inspirations such as Big Thief, Goth Babe, Beach Fossils, Frightened Rabbit and Youth Lagoon and dove into producing music of his own. item.title: LS 101: Basics of Live Sound
item.description:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Basics of Live Sound is a 2-hour introduction to live audio engineering and an orientation to Vera Project’s methods and equipment. Topics covered include an introduction to microphones and mic placement, signal paths, stands, cables, DI’s, snake, sub-snakes and etiquette for working with bands. This class is designed for both the novices and experts of all ages and will give an overview of signal flow and setting up small PAs, in addition to the basic requirements for being part of the Vera sound team at shows. Discover why the accommodating, open minded “Vera Attitude” of show production is appreciated worldwide! This class is a prerequisite for advanced audio classes at Vera and Sound Runner shifts at Vera Project show. Each Live Sound class unlocks new audio engineering volunteer shifts at Vera and eventually paid opportunities in and outside of the venue. Become a volunteer at Vera to access other audio-related educational programs such as tours, field trips, and internships!

From The Vera Project

Event details

item.title: SP 101: Intro to VERA's Screen Print Studio
item.description:
Wednesday, May 20, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

This 2-hour, comprehensive hands-on class teaches you all the basics of screen printing at VERA’s screen printing studio: From hand-drawing a stencil on transparency, coating and burning a screen, to pulling your very first print! Get comfortable with Vera's shop layout, equipment, and culture. Learn techniques for creating screens, terminology, and more to create 1-color projects in the print shop. Students will walk away with the basic skills for screen printing 1-color paper prints, and their own print of a special Vera design, specific to the 101 class. This class is a prerequisite for 200-level classes at VERA, as well as access to Open Sessions to work on your own projects in VERA’s screen printing studio.

From The Vera Project

item.title: Old Mervs, guests
item.description:
Saturday, May 23, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $25 ($31.68 after fees) Day of: $30 ($36.83 after fees) Old Mervs: An unbreakable bond shared between lifelong mates, Dave and Henry hail from the tiny farming community of Kojonup, a town three hours south of Perth, Western Australia. Starting out as school friends and rehearsing in a makeshift space in an old farm shed, Old Mervs are a far cry from their humble beginnings. Riding a wave of wins, Old Mervs are celebrating a massive 2025 after the success of their ARIA-charting debut album release and sold out tours across Australia, the United States, the UK and Europe. Recorded with ARIA award-winning producer Chris Collins, their debut self-titled album scored coveted Australian youth broadcaster triple j’s Feature Album of the week and landed at #1 on the ARIA album charts, winning hearts with hits “Parched“, “What You’ve Lost”, “Forget It”, “Everyone Will See It” and “Don’t Go”. In 2025, the pair embarked on a mammoth 20 date album tour across Australia and New Zealand along with their biggest headline home-state show to date at Perth HPC. They then headed to the US to play at world-renowned festival Lollapalooza alongside names like Olivia Rodrigo, Tyler the Creator and Sabrina Carpenter, bookended by a massive run of headline shows across the States, stopping in for a Audiotree session too. They closed out the year with a return to the UK and their first ever shows in Europe. This year looks to be just as big, if not bigger. With new music in the pipeline, and a stack of touring at home and across the globe, Old Mervs are thrilled to return to the US again for a stack of huge headline shows, along with Kilby Block Party, Gov Ball and more dates to be announced! Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

Event details

item.title: The Crocodile Presents: The Scratch, guests
item.description:
Sunday, May 24, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $23.50 ($31.93 after fees) Day of: $25 ($33.48 after fees) The Scratch: Formed in 2016 after their previous tech and groove-metal outfit Red Enemy had dissolved, The Scratch are a band that doesn’t seem to fit in anywhere, but have proven to fit in everywhere. Starting out with just acoustic guitars and a wooden box, the band created an immediate buzz in their hometown of Dublin, Ireland. Seemingly uncapable of keeping themselves boxed in to one singular genre, they meander intentionally around heavy metal, Irish folk music, stadium rock, and post punk. Constantly evolving both creatively and sonically, never resting on their laurels, never taking themselves too seriously, while always putting their souls into the music. They have won the hearts of anyone that has experienced their live shows, and have garnered a cultlike following in their native Ireland, the UK and most recently mainland Europe and North America.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Clock-Out Lounge Presents: Bob Log III with guests
item.description:
Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: Clock-Out Lounge\, 4864 Beacon Ave S\, Seattle\, 98108\, United States Of America

Clock-Out Lounge Presents: Bob Log IIIwith guests Wednesday May 27th8:00 doors 21+$17adv/ $20 dos Bob Log III is a one man band slide guitar party. A perverse product of theArizona desert\, Bob Log III has been diligently travelling the globe since 1996\,showcasing his incomparable talents and developing a devoted following along theway. A whirlwind of dexterous limbs and digits\, this super star sonic showman hasto be seen to be believed.Clad in his cannonball man\, extra tight jumpsuit and signature motorcycle helmetwith telephone/microphone attached\, Bob Log III unfailingly sweats up a riverwhile kicking on a kick drum\, stomping his homemade foot cymbal and playingslide on an old archtop guitar. His endless pursuit for musical mayhem and adownright party has seen his unique talents utilized for childrens birthday parties\,obscure beer barns\, mammoth music festivals and everything in between.Yet\, beyond these mesmerizing visuals\, lies the true reason for Bob Logsinternational cult status and superhero adoration. It is quite simply the guitarplaying – finger picked lightning\, sliding up and down\, stopping when it wants to\,then starting again when it feels like it\, all in a way that makes people moveuncontrollably\, smile and reel.___Excerpts from an unpublished journal The Adventures of Bob Log III and HisFaithful Guitar\, by The Guitar

From Clock-Out Lounge

item.title: Jean Mann Single Release Show w/ Sam n' Aimz, Dog Mom - Benifitting Gender Justice League *partially seated*
item.description:
Wednesday, May 27, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Tractor, 5213 Ballard Avenue NW, Seattle, WA

Proceeds from this show will be benefitting Gender Justice League. About Gender Justice LeagueGender Justice League is a civil & human rights organization focused on elevating the rights of TwoSpirit, Trans, & Gender Diverse (2STGD) communities in Washington state & beyond through advocacy efforts, direct services, shared leadership development, and the creation of sacred Trans spaces like Trans Pride Seattle.Founded in 2012 in Seattle, WA by a group of long-time Trans, queer, and allied activists, GJLs purpose was to increase the communitys capacity to address the cissexism, transphobia, transmisogyny, and homo/bi/queer-phobias that 2STGD people face. Recognizing that only 4% of employees in the major LGBTQ+ organizations were Trans, and that Trans people face nearly double the unemployment rate of cis people, GJLs gender justice model seeks to empower 2STGD people to combat the structural oppression, discrimination, and violence that we face in our daily lives.Social, economic, and racial justice is at the core of our work. Our analysis of gender justice focuses on issues that are often left on the sidelines of traditional LGBTQ+ activism, such as incarceration and the prison industrial complex, resisting gentrification, homelessness and housing accessibility, reproductive justice, labor rights, and unemployment. This event is 21 and over. Any ticket holder unable to present valid identification indicating that they are at least 21 years of age will not be admitted to this event, and will not be eligible for a refund. This ticket is for admission to a live music venue. It provides the holder to observe a musical performance and nothing else. Other goods and services may be purchased once inside the venue. Please note, seating is limited and is available on a first come, first served basis. Price: $25.08 - $25.08

item.title: Primitive Ring, Peyote Ugly, Screen Frogs
item.description:
Thursday, May 28, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: 5433 Ballard Avenue Northwest, Seattle, Washington 98107, United States

Advance: $12 ($15.97 after fees) Day of: $15 ($20.09 after fees) Primitive Ring: PRIMITIVE RING consists of Bert Hoover (bass/vocals), Charles Moothart (guitar/vocals), and Jon Modaff (drums). The Los Angeles power trio formed in September 2024 when Hoover and Moothart came together seeking a new creative outlet. In 2025, the band released four 7"s on four different labels (Greenway, In the Red, Reverberation Appreciation Society, and Fuzz Club UK). They hauled amps and drums into bars to hone the sound of the newfound brotherhood that is Primitive Ring. Rock and roll is in its essence a primal experience, and the name reflects that. Three individuals collide and egos rattle, shedding layers and growing new limbs. Archetypes flash to the rhythm of a freight train and the crown is passed hand to hand like a good luck charm, symbolic and communal. Rock and roll is for the people and Primitive Ring is for the taking. At least it will be when the band's debut LP is hurled into the future via In the Red Recordings on MAY 15 2026. Please note, the Sunset Tavern is a strictly 21+ bar and venue and does not accept vertical ID’s for entry.

From Sunset Tavern

item.title: Screen Printing Open Session
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 12 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

You MUST take the SP 101: Intro to Vera's Screen Print Studio class before you can sign up for an Open Session Vera’s screen print studio offers four-hour Open Sessions where individuals who have completed the introductory class may use our screen print lab to work on their own projects. Our Open Sessions are looked over by a volunteer moderator, who can provide you with advice and guidance for anything taught in our Screen Printing 101 class. We offer three different kinds of tickets at Open Sessions: Vacuum Table for paper and other flat media, T-shirt Press for shirts, tote bags and other fabrics, and Screen prep for just screen preparation. Each ticket is limited to two. If the ticket you are looking for is sold out, please add yourself to the waitlist so you can be notified when a ticket becomes available. For Open Sessions, Vera provides: - Rentable screens - Emulsion and emulsion remover - Squegees - Basic tools like spatulas, rulers, tape, etc. Participants must provide their own: - Substrate (paper, t-shirts, etc) - Water based ink Vera does not allow the use of non-water based inks at our studio, as our studio is not set up to safely process non-water based ink. If you have any questions, please email matt@theveraproject.org. FAQ 1\. Can Vera print a transparent stencil for me? Yes — You must send us your stencil at least 24 hours before the start of your session. Your file must be a .PDF, no larger than 12" x 18", and ready to print. Vera cannot make any edits to your file. Stencils are $5 each and can be paid via Debit/Credit when you arrive. If you are not interested in getting a stencil printed at Vera, you can print your own on transparency at a FedEx or similar print service location. 2\. What if I need direct assistance on my projects? Our moderators are trained to assist with any topics covered in the SP 101 class offered at Vera. While some may be able to give you basic advice on your project, they are not expected to ask as a direct instructor. If you have a project where you would like one-on-one guidance, please reach out to matt@theveraproject.org to request private tutoring. 3\. I really want to use your studio during an Open Session, but there are no slots available? Our studio is in high demand, so we apologize if our Open Session is sold out — We have limited space in the studio and several events every day at Vera, so it can be difficult for us to find more time for Open Sessions. If you're interested in booking the studio outside of Open Sessions, please email matt@theveraproject.org to discuss private rental rates and availability.

From The Vera Project

item.title: DJ 101: Intro to Digital Dejaying
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Interested in learning more about DJ-ing? In this 3-hour crash course, students will learn the ins-and-outs of Serato, playing and mixing tracks, finessing your tracks, and general tips and tricks for digital Dejaying! *Use of a laptop is required to take part in this class — Vera has a limited number of laptops available to borrow for membership. If you need one to participate, please email yewon.k@theveraproject.org to confirm our availability.

From The Vera Project

Event details

item.title: RS 101: Intro to Vera's Recording Studio
item.description:
Saturday, May 30, 2026 at 1 PM

Location: 305 Harrison Street, Seattle, Washington 98109, United States

Get into VERA’s Recording Studio through this 2-hour workshop and studio orientation! Learn the studio infrastructure including, bootup, signalflow, patchbay setup, mixing desk operation, and basic Digital Audio Workstation (DAW) integration. During this session, participants will experiment with different ways of running signals from the live room to the computer. Some familiarity with Digital Audio Workstations (i.e. ProTools, Logic, Reaper, Ableton, etc.) is encouraged. After taking this course, students who are new to studio recording should be ready for the multi-week Recording Studio Workshop Series, as well as having a better grounding for independent research. Students who are experienced with studio recording may be credentialed for volunteering at studio sessions.

From The Vera Project
